AddCollectionRecordsPayload.description:
Payload for adding records to a collection.

Records are added in batches. All arrays must have the same length, with each index
representing a single record. For example, `ids[0]`, `embeddings[0]`, `documents[0]`, etc.
all belong to the same record.

embeddings.description: Embeddings for each record. Can contain the raw f32 arrays or base64 encoded strings.

created.description: True if newly created, false if already existed (idempotent request).

AttachedFunctionApiResponse.description: API response struct for attached function with function_name instead of function_id

completion_offset.description: Completion offset: the WAL position up to which the attached function has processed records

database_id.description: Database name this attached function belongs to

id.description: Unique identifier for the attached function

input_collection_id.description: Source collection that triggers the attached function

min_records_for_invocation.description: Minimum number of new records required before the attached function runs again

name.description: Human-readable name for the attached function instance

output_collection.description: Name of target collection where attached function output is stored

oneOf.description: ID of the output collection (lazily filled in after creation)

params.description: Optional JSON parameters for the function

tenant_id.description: Tenant name this attached function belongs to

id.description: Unique identifier for the attached function.

name.description: Human-readable name for the attached function instance.

AttachedFunctionUuid.description: AttachedFunctionUuid is a wrapper around Uuid to provide a type for attached function identifiers.

DeleteCollectionRecordsPayload.description:
Payload for deleting records from a collection.

Records can be deleted by their IDs or by a metadata filter. At least one of `ids` or `where`
must be provided.

delete_output.description: Whether to delete the output collection as well when detaching the function.

FloatListValueType.description: Float list value type index configurations (for vectors)

GetRequestPayload.description:
Records can be retrieved by their IDs or by a metadata filter. At least one of `ids` or `where`
must be provided. Use `include` to specify which fields to return in the response.

GetResponse.description:
All arrays have the same length, with each index representing a single record.
Only fields specified in the request's `include` parameter are populated.

include.description: List of fields that were included in this response.

HnswIndexConfig.description: Configuration for HNSW vector index algorithm parameters

Include.description: Use this enum to specify which fields should be returned when retrieving records.

Quantization.description: Quantization implementation for SPANN vector index.

Schema.description:
Schema representation for collection index configurations

This represents the server-side schema structure used for index management

cmek.description: Customer-managed encryption key for collection data

defaults.description: Default index configurations for each value type

keys.description:
Key-specific index overrides
TODO(Sanket): Needed for backwards compatibility. Should remove after deploy.

source_attached_function_id.description: ID of the attached function that created this output collection (if applicable)

read_level.description: Specifies whether to include unindexed data in the search results.

SpannIndexConfig.description: Configuration for SPANN vector index algorithm parameters

quantize.description: Quantization implementation for vector search (cloud-only feature)

SparseVector.description:
Represents a sparse vector using parallel arrays for indices and values.

On deserialization: accepts both old format `{"indices": [...], "values": [...]}`
and new format `{"#type": "sparse_vector", "indices": [...], "values": [...]}`.

On serialization: always includes `#type` field with value `"sparse_vector"`.

source_key.description: Key to source the sparse vector from

SparseVectorValueType.description: Sparse vector value type index configurations

UpdateCollectionRecordsPayload.description:
Payload for updating existing records in a collection.

Records are added in batches. All arrays must have the same length, with each index
representing a single record. For example, `ids[0]`, `embeddings[0]`, `documents[0]`, etc.
all belong to the same record.

oneOf.description: Updated embeddings for each record. Can contain the raw f32 arrays or base64 encoded strings.

UpsertCollectionRecordsPayload.description:
Payload for upserting records in a collection.

Upsert creates records if they don't exist, or updates them if they do.
Records are added in batches. All arrays must have the same length, with each index
representing a single record. For example, `ids[0]`, `embeddings[0]`, `documents[0]`, etc.
all belong to the same record.

embeddings.description: Embeddings for each record. Can contain the raw f32 arrays or base64 encoded strings.

ValueTypes.description:
Strongly-typed value type configurations
Contains optional configurations for each supported value type

source_key.description: Key to source the vector from

oneOf.description: Vector space for similarity calculation (cosine, l2, ip)

get.description: Returns the current user's identity, tenant, and databases.
Returns a collection by Chroma Resource Name.
Returns the health status of the service.
Returns a nanosecond timestamp of the current time.
Returns an existing tenant by name.

patch.description: Updates an existing tenant by name.

get.description: Lists all databases for a tenant.

post.description: Creates a new database for a tenant.

get.description: Lists all collections in a database.

x-codeSamples.label: List collections
x-codeSamples.source: const collections = await client.listCollections({ limit: 10, offset: 0 });

post.description: Creates a new collection in a database.

delete.description: Deletes a collection in a database.

get.description: Returns a collection by ID or name.

put.description: Updates an existing collection's name or metadata.

x-codeSamples.label: Update collection
Add records
x-codeSamples.source:
collection.add(
    vec!["id1".to_string(), "id2".to_string()],
    vec![vec![0.1, 0.2], vec![0.3, 0.4]],
    Some(vec![Some("doc1".to_string()), Some("doc2".to_string())]),
    None,
    None
).await?;

post.description: Detaches a function from a collection.

get.description: Returns the number of records in a collection.

parameters.description: Read level for consistency vs performance tradeoffs
parameters.name: read_level

200.description: Number of records in the collection

post.description: Deletes records in a collection. Can filter by IDs or metadata.

x-codeSamples.label: Delete records by metadata filter

post.description: Creates a fork of an existing collection.
Attaches a function to a collection.

get.description: Returns an attached function by name.

post.description: Returns records from a collection by ID or metadata filter.

x-codeSamples.label: Get records by metadata filter
x-codeSamples.source: const results = await collection.get({ where: { category: 'science' }, limit: 10 });

x-codeSamples.label: Get records by IDs
Get records by metadata filter

get.description: Returns the indexing status of a collection.

post.description: Queries a collection using dense vector search with metadata and full-text search filtering.

x-codeSamples.label: Query with embeddings
x-codeSamples.source: const results = await collection.query({ queryEmbeddings: [[0.1, 0.2, 0.3]], nResults: 10 });

x-codeSamples.label: Query with text
x-codeSamples.source: const results = await collection.query({ queryTexts: ['search text'], nResults: 10 });

x-codeSamples.label: Query with embeddings
Query with metadata filter

post.description: Searches records from a collection with dense, sparse, or hybrid vector search.

x-codeSamples.label: Search with embeddings
x-codeSamples.source:
import { Search, K, Knn } from 'chromadb';
const results = await collection.search(new Search().rank(Knn({ query: [0.1, 0.2, 0.3], limit: 10 })));

x-codeSamples.label: Search with embeddings
x-codeSamples.source:
from chromadb import Search, Knn
search = Search().rank(Knn(query=[0.1, 0.2, 0.3], limit=10))
results = collection.search(search)

x-codeSamples.label: Search with embeddings
Search with metadata filter

post.description: Updates records in a collection by ID.

x-codeSamples.label: Update records
x-codeSamples.source: await collection.update({ ids: ['id1'], documents: ['updated doc'], metadatas: [{ key: 'value' }] });

x-codeSamples.label: Update records

post.description: Upserts records in a collection (create if not exists, otherwise update).

x-codeSamples.label: Upsert records
x-codeSamples.source:
collection.upsert(
    vec!["id1".to_string(), "id2".to_string()],
    vec![vec![0.1, 0.2], vec![0.3, 0.4]],
    Some(vec![Some("doc1".to_string()), Some("doc2".to_string())]),
    None,
    None
).await?;

get.description: Returns the total number of collections in a database.
Returns the version of the server.
